what_it_does: A CSS stylesheet for the 'Vi UI kit' that implements a fixed three-pane workspace: a branded sidebar shell, a central layered task tree, and a right-hand chat/composer with a live output preview (brochure). It defines layout grids, component states, animations, and a gold/bronze themed visual vocabulary using CSS custom properties.
observations: Uses CSS custom properties extensively for colors, fonts, radii, shadows, and easing.
Workspace is a rigid three-column grid: 380px / 1fr / 420px with 100vh height.
Shell sidebar is 240px wide and contains brand, navigation headings, and nav items.
Active nav items use a dashed border and reduced padding, causing a slight layout shift.
Status pills, task nodes, and read cards share a gold-accented state language (idle/active/done/blocked).
Task tree uses layer-specific grids: layer-1 = 1 column, layer-2 = 2 columns, layer-3 = 4 columns.
Includes several keyframe animations: pulsing dot, pulsing pill, skeleton shimmer, and Vi stripe.
Brochure preview has a fixed 140px gradient hero and a four-column feature grid.
No media queries or responsive breakpoints are present.
Some values are hardcoded (hero gradient colors, blocked node background #FBEEEC) rather than using CSS variables.
standouts: The three-pane workspace grid and its component coverage in a single CSS file.
Warm gold/bronze accent theme applied consistently across status, chat, tasks, and preview.
Animated liveness indicators (pulsing dot, pulsing pill, skeleton placeholders).
Layered task tree with column-count scaling per layer depth.
conventions: Component classes use a 'vi-' prefix in a BEM-like flat naming scheme.
CSS custom properties drive theming (colors, typography, radii, shadows, easing).
Layout relies on CSS Grid and Flexbox with fixed pixel dimensions.
Section comments group related components (Shell, Workspace, Chat panel, Task tree, Output preview).
Transitions and keyframe animations are co-located with their components.
concerns: Fixed pixel widths (240px, 380px, 420px) and no media queries make the layout non-responsive.
body { overflow: hidden } may trap focus or hide content on smaller viewports.
.vi-nav-item.active changes border and padding simultaneously, producing a small layout shift.
Hardcoded colors in the brochure hero gradient and .vi-node.blocked background break the variable-driven theme.
No fallback values for CSS custom properties.
Both .vi-shell and .vi-workspace set height: 100vh; their intended nesting relationship is unclear from this file alone.
notes: This file is the complete visual skin for a gold-accented, fixed three-pane assistant workspace covering navigation, chat, task tracking, and a live preview card.
questions: Where are the CSS custom properties (var(--font-body), var(--accent-gold), etc.) defined?
Is .vi-shell meant to wrap .vi-workspace, or are they alternative root layouts?
Are the SVG glyphs and avatar icons supplied in a companion file or inline?
What font families do --font-body and --font-display map to?
Is there companion JavaScript that toggles the active/done/blocked task states and drives the skeleton placeholders?
purpose_vs_actual: The header says 'Vi UI kit — three-pane workspace' and the file indeed defines a three-pane workspace with sidebar, task tree, and chat/preview panes, so the stated purpose matches the content.
apparent_themes: Warm metallic accent palette (gold/bronze) for a premium, assistant-like feel.
Status-driven visual feedback at every level (nav, chat, tasks, preview).
Layered decomposition of work into hierarchical task nodes.
Preview-as-you-go with placeholder and completion states.
Subtle motion to signal liveness and progress.
intention_signals: The stylesheet appears to support a conversational AI/agent interface named Vi, where a user chats in the right pane, watches a multi-layer task plan execute in the center pane, and sees a generated document or brochure preview update in real time.
novelty: It combines chat, a layered task tree with per-layer column layouts, and a live brochure preview into one cohesive, themeable CSS file, supported by a distinctive gold-accented status language and custom animations.
contribution: A self-contained reference implementation of a three-pane assistant workspace UI, including layout grids, component states, chat/composer styling, task-node hierarchy, and preview chrome, which a fused system could keep as the Vi UI kit's visual specification.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS stylesheet for the Vi UI kit implementing a fixed three-pane workspace with a sidebar, layered task tree, chat/composer, and live brochure preview using a gold/bronze theme and CSS custom properties.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the visual layout and component styling for a conversational agent workspace where users chat, monitor layered task execution, and review generated output.
